seniority.level: Staff IC (likely Staff PM / IC6 equivalent)
level_signals: 8+ years of experience in software engineering, ML engineering, or a deeply technical product role
Expected to own product roadmap and drive outcomes across engineering, operations, and go-to-market

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in software engineering, ML engineering, or a deeply technical product role where you were hands-on with what shipped
Technical fluency: software engineering or ML background (master's degree in computer science or equivalent experience).
Own the product roadmap and execution for one or more product areas within Global Defense, driving what gets built and how it ships
Be hands-on: write specs, prototype solutions, dig into technical architecture with engineers, and make product calls grounded in real technical understanding
Design and ship AI-powered products and tooling for defense and national security workflows, working side-by-side with engineering and ML teams
Use customer context to inform what you build — understand allied defense workflows deeply enough to make opinionated product decisions, not just relay requirements
Navigate the unique constraints of defense product development across allied nations — classification environments, accreditation processes, air-gapped deployments — or be ready to learn these fast
Operate like a founding team member: wear multiple hats, move fast, unblock yourself, and drive outcomes across engineering, operations, and go-to-market
Deep intellectual curiosity about AI systems — you read papers, dig into technical details, and have strong opinions about how things should be built
Willingness to travel to allied partner nations as needed

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience training or evaluating models is a plus

scope.surface: Scale's Global Defense products for allied defense and national security customers
scope.business_model: B2G (government / defense), AI data and full-stack technology platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Product roadmap execution and delivery velocity
Adoption and impact of AI-powered defense products
Customer mission outcomes for allied defense partners
core_responsibilities: Own the product roadmap and execution for one or more product areas within Global Defense
Write specs, prototype solutions, dig into technical architecture with engineers
Design and ship AI-powered products and tooling for defense and national security workflows
Understand allied defense workflows to make opinionated product decisions
Navigate defense product constraints: classification environments, accreditation processes, air-gapped deployments
Operate like a founding team member across engineering, operations, and go-to-market

green_flags: Very clear product surface and ownership scope described (Global Defense product areas)
Scale AI is a well-funded, high-profile AI company with marquee government customers (U.S. Army, Air Force, Government of Qatar)
Role is explicitly high-impact and mission-critical within a fast-growing team
JD is honest about the unique constraints (air-gapped, classification) and signals willingness to train on them
red_flags: Location not specified anywhere in the JD — remote eligibility, city, and work authorization are entirely unclear
Defense work likely requires security clearance (not explicitly stated, but implied by 'classification environments'); this is a significant unstated requirement
Travel to allied partner nations is required but frequency and scope are undefined
All requirements are listed under 'Ideally, You'd Have' language, making hard vs. soft classification ambiguous throughout

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff' in title and 8+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and GPS team context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
ambiguities: All requirements appear under 'Ideally, You'd Have' header, which typically signals nice-to-haves. However, the specificity, seniority level, and unconditional phrasing of most bullets (especially '8+ years', technical fluency, and builder mentality) suggest they are effectively hard requirements. Classified as hard where language is unconditional; only the explicit 'is a plus' bullet classified as soft.
Security clearance eligibility is strongly implied by 'classification environments' and defense customer context but never explicitly stated — could be a hard requirement not disclosed.
Work location and remote eligibility are never mentioned in the JD. The role may require on-site presence at classified facilities given the defense context.
Work authorization requirements are unclear; defense roles often require U.S. citizenship but this JD does not state it explicitly.
